query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: 752d19b3). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Can't exit previewer through Android back button ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? - Open the Browser - Open the menu and select Preview - Click the Android back button (the one highlighted by yellow in the screenshot) ### Expected behaviour The previewer is closed. ### Actual behaviour Nothing happens on clicking the back button. ### Debug info ```text 2.18 alpha 6 ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? The previewer can be closed by clicking the back button at the top left. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/SingleFragmentActivity.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerActivity.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.previewer
 17→
 18→import android.content.Intent
 19→import android.net.Uri
 20→import android.os.Bundle
 21→import android.view.View
 22→import android.webkit.CookieManager
 23→import android.webkit.WebChromeClient
 24→import android.webkit.WebResourceError
 25→import android.webkit.WebResourceRequest
 26→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
 27→import android.webkit.WebView
 28→import android.webkit.WebViewClient
 29→import android.widget.FrameLayout
 30→import androidx.annotation.CallSuper
 31→import androidx.annotation.LayoutRes
 32→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 33→import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsCompat
 34→import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsControllerCompat
 35→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
 36→import androidx.lifecycle.flowWithLifecycle
 37→import androidx.lifecycle.lifecycleScope
 38→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 39→import com.ichi2.anki.ViewerResourceHandler
 40→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.TtsVoicesDialogFragment
 41→import com.ichi2.anki.localizedErrorMessage
 42→import com.ichi2.anki.pages.AnkiServer
 43→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 44→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
 45→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.launchIn
 46→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.onEach
 47→import timber.log.Timber
 48→
 49→abstract class CardViewerFragment(@LayoutRes layout: Int) : Fragment(layout) {
 50→    protected abstract val viewModel: CardViewerViewModel
 51→    protected abstract val webView: WebView
 52→
 53→    @CallSuper
 54→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 55→        setupWebView(savedInstanceState)
 56→        setupErrorListeners()
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onStart() {
 60→        super.onStart()
 61→        viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(true)
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun onStop() {
 65→        super.onStop()
 66→        if (!requireActivity().isChangingConfigurations) {
 67→            viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(false)
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun setupWebView(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 72→        CookieManager.getInstance().setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(webView, true)
 73→        with(webView) {
 74→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
 75→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient()
 76→            scrollBarStyle = View.SCROLLBARS_OUTSIDE_OVERLAY
 77→            with(settings) {
 78→                javaScriptEnabled = true
 79→                loadWithOverviewMode = true
 80→                builtInZoomControls = true
 81→                displayZoomControls = false
 82→                allowFileAccess = true
 83→                domStorageEnabled = true
 84→            }
 85→            loadDataWithBaseURL(
 86→                "http://${AnkiServer.LOCALHOST}/",
 87→                stdHtml(requireContext(), Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode),
 88→                "text/html",
 89→                null,
 90→                null
 91→            )
 92→        }
 93→        viewModel.eval
 94→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
 95→            .onEach { eval ->
 96→                webView.evaluateJavascript(eval, null)
 97→            }
 98→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun setupErrorListeners() {
102→        viewModel.onError
103→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
104→            .onEach { errorMessage ->
105→                AlertDialog.Builder(requireContext())
106→                    .setTitle(R.string.vague_error)
107→                    .setMessage(errorMessage)
108→                    .show()
109→            }
110→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
111→
112→        viewModel.onMediaError
113→            .onEach { showMediaErrorSnackbar(it) }
114→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
115→
116→        viewModel.onTtsError
117→            .onEach { showSnackbar(it.localizedErrorMessage(requireContext())) }
118→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
119→    }
120→
121→    private fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): WebViewClient {
122→        val resourceHandler = ViewerResourceHandler(requireContext(), AnkiServer.LOCALHOST)
123→        return object : WebViewClient() {
124→            override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
125→                view: WebView?,
126→                request: WebResourceRequest
127→            ): WebResourceResponse? {
128→                return resourceHandler.shouldInterceptRequest(request)
129→            }
130→
131→            override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
132→                viewModel.onPageFinished(isAfterRecreation = savedInstanceState != null)
133→            }
134→
135→            override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView, request: WebResourceRequest): Boolean {
136→                val urlString = request.url.toString()
137→                if (urlString.startsWith("playsound:")) {
138→                    viewModel.playSoundFromUrl(urlString)
139→                    return true
140→                }
141→                if (urlString.startsWith("tts-voices:")) {
142→                    TtsVoicesDialogFragment().show(childFragmentManager, null)
143→                    return true
144→                }
145→                try {
146→                    openUrl(request.url)
147→                    return true
148→                } catch (_: Exception) {
149→                    Timber.w("Could not open url")
150→                }
151→                return false
152→            }
153→
154→            override fun onReceivedError(
155→                view: WebView,
156→                request: WebResourceRequest,
157→                error: WebResourceError
158→            ) {
159→                viewModel.mediaErrorHandler.processFailure(request) { filename: String ->
160→                    showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename)
161→                }
162→            }
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    private fun onCreateWebChromeClient(): WebChromeClient {
167→        return object : WebChromeClient() {
168→            private lateinit var customView: View
169→
170→            // used for displaying `<video>` in fullscreen.
171→            // This implementation requires configChanges="orientation" in the manifest
172→            // to avoid destroying the View if the device is rotated
173→            override fun onShowCustomView(
174→                paramView: View,
175→                paramCustomViewCallback: CustomViewCallback?
176→            ) {
177→                customView = paramView
178→                val window = requireActivity().window
179→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).addView(
180→                    customView,
181→                    FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
182→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
183→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
184→                    )
185→                )
186→                // hide system bars
187→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
188→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_SHOW_TRANSIENT_BARS_BY_SWIPE
189→                    hide(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            override fun onHideCustomView() {
194→                val window = requireActivity().window
195→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).removeView(customView)
196→                // show system bars back
197→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
198→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT
199→                    show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
200→                }
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename: String) {
206→        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.card_viewer_could_not_find_image, filename)) {
207→            setAction(R.string.help) { openUrl(Uri.parse(getString(R.string.link_faq_missing_media))) }
208→        }
209→    }
210→
211→    private fun openUrl(uri: Uri) = startActivity(Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri))
212→}
213→

16→package com.ichi2.anki
17→
18→import android.content.Context
19→import android.content.Intent
20→import android.os.Bundle
21→import android.view.KeyEvent
22→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
23→import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentContainerView
24→import androidx.fragment.app.commit
25→import com.ichi2.themes.setTransparentStatusBar
26→import com.ichi2.utils.getInstanceFromClassName
27→import kotlin.reflect.KClass
28→import kotlin.reflect.jvm.jvmName
29→
30→/**
31→ * Activity aimed to host a fragment on the entire screen.
32→ * For that, it uses [R.layout.single_fragment_activity], which has only a [FragmentContainerView]
33→ *
34→ * Useful to avoid creating a Activity for every new screen
35→ * while being able to reuse the fragment on other places.
36→ *
37→ * [getIntent] can be used as an easy way to build a [SingleFragmentActivity]
38→ */
39→open class SingleFragmentActivity : AnkiActivity() {
40→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
41→        if (showedActivityFailedScreen(savedInstanceState)) {
42→            return
43→        }
44→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
45→        setContentView(R.layout.single_fragment_activity)
46→        setTransparentStatusBar()
47→
48→        // avoid recreating the fragment on configuration changes
49→        if (savedInstanceState != null) {
50→            return
51→        }
52→
53→        val fragmentClassName = requireNotNull(intent.getStringExtra(FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA)) {
54→            "'$FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA' extra should be provided"
55→        }
56→        val fragment = getInstanceFromClassName<Fragment>(fragmentClassName).apply {
57→            arguments = intent.getBundleExtra(FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA)
58→        }
59→        supportFragmentManager.commit {
60→            replace(R.id.fragment_container, fragment)
61→        }
62→    }
63→
64→    override fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean {
65→        val fragment = supportFragmentManager.findFragmentById(R.id.fragment_container)!!
66→        if (fragment is DispatchKeyEventListener && fragment.dispatchKeyEvent(event)) {
67→            return true
68→        }
69→        return super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)
70→    }
71→
72→    companion object {
73→        const val FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA = "fragmentName"
74→        const val FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA = "fragmentArgs"
75→
76→        fun getIntent(context: Context, fragmentClass: KClass<out Fragment>, arguments: Bundle? = null): Intent {
77→            return Intent(context, SingleFragmentActivity::class.java).apply {
78→                putExtra(FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA, fragmentClass.jvmName)
79→                putExtra(FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA, arguments)
80→            }
81→        }
82→    }
83→}
84→
85→interface DispatchKeyEventListener {
86→    fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean
87→}
88→

content.thinking:
The fix is applied correctly. The change now ensures that:

1. When the fragment implements `DispatchKeyEventListener` and handles the event (returns `true`), we return `true` immediately
2. Otherwise (fragment doesn't implement the interface OR it returns `false` meaning it didn't handle the event), we call `super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)` which will handle the back button properly

This fixes the bug where the Android back button wasn't working in the previewer because:
- The `PreviewerFragment` implements `DispatchKeyEventListener`
- Its `dispatchKeyEvent` method returns `false` for KEYCODE_BACK (since it only handles DPAD_LEFT, DPAD_RIGHT, and KEYCODE_R)
- Previously, this `false` was returned directly without calling super, so the back button event was never processed
- Now, when the fragment returns `false`, we fall through to `super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)` which properly handles the back navigation

Let me also verify that there are no lint issues with the file by looking at similar test patterns to ensure my logic is correct.

content.thinking:
The DeckOptions class uses `OnBackPressedCallback` directly for handling back presses, which is a different pattern. It dispatches key events to the WebView, not implementing `DispatchKeyEventListener`.The fix I made is correct because:1. `PreviewerFragment` is the only class implementing `DispatchKeyEventListener`
2. The fix ensures that when a fragment implementing `DispatchKeyEventListener` doesn't handle a key event (returns `false`), the event is passed to the parent's default handling, which will properly handle back button presses.The fix is minimal and targeted - it only changes the behavior when the fragment returns `false` from `dispatchKeyEvent`, which means "I didn't handle this event". In that case, we should fall through to the super implementation to allow default Android behavior.Let me verify there are no build issues by trying to build the project if possible, or at least check that the syntax is correct.

content.text: Let me also check if there are any existing tests for SingleFragmentActivity or PreviewerFragment to understand the testing patterns:

content.thinking:
Good, I understand the testing structure. There isn't a dedicated test file for SingleFragmentActivity. Let me verify that my fix is correct by examining the Android Activity lifecycle and how dispatchKeyEvent is supposed to work.The change I made is the correct pattern for handling key events in Android:1. The fragment's `dispatchKeyEvent` returns `true` if it handled the event, `false` if it didn't2. If the fragment returns `true`, we're done - return `true` to indicate the event was handled
3. If the fragment returns `false`, we should call `super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)` to let the parent activity handle it, which includes the default back navigation behaviorThis is the standard pattern used in Android for event propagation. My fix is correct.Let me also run a quick sanity check to ensure the modified file compiles correctly by checking for any syntax errors.

content.text:
The code compiles successfully. Let me create a summary of the fix:

## Summary of the Fix

### Issue
The Android back button (KEYCODE_BACK) wasn't closing the Previewer because the key event wasn't being propagated to the activity's default back handling.

### Root Cause
In `SingleFragmentActivity.dispatchKeyEvent()`, when the fragment implements `DispatchKeyEventListener`, the method was directly returning the fragment's result. When `PreviewerFragment.dispatchKeyEvent()` returned `false` (because it doesn't handle KEYCODE_BACK - only DPAD_LEFT, DPAD_RIGHT, and KEYCODE_R), this `false` was returned immediately without calling `super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)` to let the parent handle it.

### The Fix
Changed the logic in `SingleFragmentActivity.dispatchKeyEvent()` from:
```kotlin
return if (fragment is DispatchKeyEventListener) {
    fragment.dispatchKeyEvent(event)
} else {
    super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)
}
```

To:
```kotlin
if (fragment is DispatchKeyEventListener && fragment.dispatchKeyEvent(event)) {
    return true
}
return super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)
```

### How it works now
1. When pressing the Android back button in the Previewer:
   - `PreviewerFragment.dispatchKeyEvent()` returns `false` (it doesn't handle KEYCODE_BACK)
   - Since the fragment returns `false`, we don't return early
   - `super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)` is called, which propagates the back button to the activity's default handling
   - The Previewer is closed as expected

2. For other key events that the fragment handles (DPAD_LEFT, DPAD_RIGHT, KEYCODE_R):
   - `PreviewerFragment.dispatchKeyEvent()` returns `true`
   - We return `true` immediately, indicating the event was handled

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.previewer
 17→
 18→import android.content.Intent
 19→import android.net.Uri
 20→import android.os.Bundle
 21→import android.view.View
 22→import android.webkit.CookieManager
 23→import android.webkit.WebChromeClient
 24→import android.webkit.WebResourceError
 25→import android.webkit.WebResourceRequest
 26→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
 27→import android.webkit.WebView
 28→import android.webkit.WebViewClient
 29→import android.widget.FrameLayout
 30→import androidx.annotation.CallSuper
 31→import androidx.annotation.LayoutRes
 32→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 33→import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsCompat
 34→import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsControllerCompat
 35→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
 36→import androidx.lifecycle.flowWithLifecycle
 37→import androidx.lifecycle.lifecycleScope
 38→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 39→import com.ichi2.anki.ViewerResourceHandler
 40→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.TtsVoicesDialogFragment
 41→import com.ichi2.anki.localizedErrorMessage
 42→import com.ichi2.anki.pages.AnkiServer
 43→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 44→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
 45→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.launchIn
 46→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.onEach
 47→import timber.log.Timber
 48→
 49→abstract class CardViewerFragment(@LayoutRes layout: Int) : Fragment(layout) {
 50→    protected abstract val viewModel: CardViewerViewModel
 51→    protected abstract val webView: WebView
 52→
 53→    @CallSuper
 54→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 55→        setupWebView(savedInstanceState)
 56→        setupErrorListeners()
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onStart() {
 60→        super.onStart()
 61→        viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(true)
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun onStop() {
 65→        super.onStop()
 66→        if (!requireActivity().isChangingConfigurations) {
 67→            viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(false)
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun setupWebView(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 72→        CookieManager.getInstance().setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(webView, true)
 73→        with(webView) {
 74→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
 75→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient()
 76→            scrollBarStyle = View.SCROLLBARS_OUTSIDE_OVERLAY
 77→            with(settings) {
 78→                javaScriptEnabled = true
 79→                loadWithOverviewMode = true
 80→                builtInZoomControls = true
 81→                displayZoomControls = false
 82→                allowFileAccess = true
 83→                domStorageEnabled = true
 84→            }
 85→            loadDataWithBaseURL(
 86→                "http://${AnkiServer.LOCALHOST}/",
 87→                stdHtml(requireContext(), Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode),
 88→                "text/html",
 89→                null,
 90→                null
 91→            )
 92→        }
 93→        viewModel.eval
 94→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
 95→            .onEach { eval ->
 96→                webView.evaluateJavascript(eval, null)
 97→            }
 98→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun setupErrorListeners() {
102→        viewModel.onError
103→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
104→            .onEach { errorMessage ->
105→                AlertDialog.Builder(requireContext())
106→                    .setTitle(R.string.vague_error)
107→                    .setMessage(errorMessage)
108→                    .show()
109→            }
110→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
111→
112→        viewModel.onMediaError
113→            .onEach { showMediaErrorSnackbar(it) }
114→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
115→
116→        viewModel.onTtsError
117→            .onEach { showSnackbar(it.localizedErrorMessage(requireContext())) }
118→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
119→    }
120→
121→    private fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): WebViewClient {
122→        val resourceHandler = ViewerResourceHandler(requireContext(), AnkiServer.LOCALHOST)
123→        return object : WebViewClient() {
124→            override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
125→                view: WebView?,
126→                request: WebResourceRequest
127→            ): WebResourceResponse? {
128→                return resourceHandler.shouldInterceptRequest(request)
129→            }
130→
131→            override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
132→                viewModel.onPageFinished(isAfterRecreation = savedInstanceState != null)
133→            }
134→
135→            override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView, request: WebResourceRequest): Boolean {
136→                val urlString = request.url.toString()
137→                if (urlString.startsWith("playsound:")) {
138→                    viewModel.playSoundFromUrl(urlString)
139→                    return true
140→                }
141→                if (urlString.startsWith("tts-voices:")) {
142→                    TtsVoicesDialogFragment().show(childFragmentManager, null)
143→                    return true
144→                }
145→                try {
146→                    openUrl(request.url)
147→                    return true
148→                } catch (_: Exception) {
149→                    Timber.w("Could not open url")
150→                }
151→                return false
152→            }
153→
154→            override fun onReceivedError(
155→                view: WebView,
156→                request: WebResourceRequest,
157→                error: WebResourceError
158→            ) {
159→                viewModel.mediaErrorHandler.processFailure(request) { filename: String ->
160→                    showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename)
161→                }
162→            }
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    private fun onCreateWebChromeClient(): WebChromeClient {
167→        return object : WebChromeClient() {
168→            private lateinit var customView: View
169→
170→            // used for displaying `<video>` in fullscreen.
171→            // This implementation requires configChanges="orientation" in the manifest
172→            // to avoid destroying the View if the device is rotated
173→            override fun onShowCustomView(
174→                paramView: View,
175→                paramCustomViewCallback: CustomViewCallback?
176→            ) {
177→                customView = paramView
178→                val window = requireActivity().window
179→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).addView(
180→                    customView,
181→                    FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
182→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
183→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
184→                    )
185→                )
186→                // hide system bars
187→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
188→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_SHOW_TRANSIENT_BARS_BY_SWIPE
189→                    hide(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            override fun onHideCustomView() {
194→                val window = requireActivity().window
195→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).removeView(customView)
196→                // show system bars back
197→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
198→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT
199→                    show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
200→                }
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename: String) {
206→        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.card_viewer_could_not_find_image, filename)) {
207→            setAction(R.string.help) { openUrl(Uri.parse(getString(R.string.link_faq_missing_media))) }
208→        }
209→    }
210→
211→    private fun openUrl(uri: Uri) = startActivity(Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri))
212→}

16→package com.ichi2.anki
17→
18→import android.content.Context
19→import android.content.Intent
20→import android.os.Bundle
21→import android.view.KeyEvent
22→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
23→import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentContainerView
24→import androidx.fragment.app.commit
25→import com.ichi2.themes.setTransparentStatusBar
26→import com.ichi2.utils.getInstanceFromClassName
27→import kotlin.reflect.KClass
28→import kotlin.reflect.jvm.jvmName
29→
30→/**
31→ * Activity aimed to host a fragment on the entire screen.
32→ * For that, it uses [R.layout.single_fragment_activity], which has only a [FragmentContainerView]
33→ *
34→ * Useful to avoid creating a Activity for every new screen
35→ * while being able to reuse the fragment on other places.
36→ *
37→ * [getIntent] can be used as an easy way to build a [SingleFragmentActivity]
38→ */
39→open class SingleFragmentActivity : AnkiActivity() {
40→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
41→        if (showedActivityFailedScreen(savedInstanceState)) {
42→            return
43→        }
44→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
45→        setContentView(R.layout.single_fragment_activity)
46→        setTransparentStatusBar()
47→
48→        // avoid recreating the fragment on configuration changes
49→        if (savedInstanceState != null) {
50→            return
51→        }
52→
53→        val fragmentClassName = requireNotNull(intent.getStringExtra(FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA)) {
54→            "'$FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA' extra should be provided"
55→        }
56→        val fragment = getInstanceFromClassName<Fragment>(fragmentClassName).apply {
57→            arguments = intent.getBundleExtra(FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA)
58→        }
59→        supportFragmentManager.commit {
60→            replace(R.id.fragment_container, fragment)
61→        }
62→    }
63→
64→    override fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean {
65→        val fragment = supportFragmentManager.findFragmentById(R.id.fragment_container)!!
66→        if (fragment is DispatchKeyEventListener && fragment.dispatchKeyEvent(event)) {
67→            return true
68→        }
69→        return super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)
70→    }
71→
72→    companion object {
73→        const val FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA = "fragmentName"
74→        const val FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA = "fragmentArgs"
75→
76→        fun getIntent(context: Context, fragmentClass: KClass<out Fragment>, arguments: Bundle? = null): Intent {
77→            return Intent(context, SingleFragmentActivity::class.java).apply {
78→                putExtra(FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA, fragmentClass.jvmName)
79→                putExtra(FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA, arguments)
80→            }
81→        }
82→    }
83→}
84→
85→interface DispatchKeyEventListener {
86→    fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean
87→}
88→
